capability_objective: The program office is reconciling two tasks for the cross-team status note. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the task named “Carrier Scorecard Refresh” at “Logistics Program” and the task named “Returns Workflow Pilot” at “Commerce Program”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write one compact sentence per record in the requested order, followed by a third sentence that compares only their statuses.
prompt:
The program office is reconciling two tasks for the cross-team status note. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable task registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness.

The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale or partial matches. Find the task named “Carrier Scorecard Refresh” at “Logistics Program” and the task named “Returns Workflow Pilot” at “Commerce Program”. Every reported value must be grounded through the registry: search by the exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first task-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get, never from search output.

This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its backing data, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete registry audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts in the workspace.

Write the complete result to status_note.md and no other new file. The file must contain exactly three newline-terminated compact sentences, one per line. The first two sentences must cover the requested tasks in the requested order. For a resolved branch, use `<name> at <location> has status <status>.`, preserving the status exactly as returned by its complete record. For an unresolved branch, use `<name> at <location> is unresolved.` When both statuses were retrieved, make the third sentence exactly `The statuses match.` or `The statuses differ.`, as appropriate. Otherwise make it exactly `A status comparison is unavailable because at least one task is unresolved.` Add no heading, bullet, label, blank line, note, recommendation, stable ID, evidence, or unrelated match. After writing the file, return exactly the same content as the final response.
